pattern: For each element treated as the subarray minimum, the best subarray is the maximal window where that element stays the minimum; monotonic stacks find those window boundaries and prefix sums give the window sum in constant time.
hints: If you fix which element is the smallest one in the chosen subarray, the most profitable subarray is the widest contiguous span in which that element is still the minimum.
Use a stack to find, for every position, how far left and right you can extend before hitting a strictly smaller value; that span is the candidate window for that element as the minimum.
Precompute prefix sums so each window's sum is one subtraction, multiply by the window's minimum, track the maximum, and only then take the result modulo 1000000007.

edge_inputs.description: single element is its own subarray
all equal so the whole array is best
strictly increasing favors a wide window with a small minimum or a narrow one with a large value
large values that approach the 64-bit product limit
a tall spike is worse than a wide moderate plateau
a valley splits two plateaus so the best window is on one side

stress.description: random small values to create many ties and nested windows
stress.note: small alphabet stresses equal-minimum boundary handling

stress.description: random wide-range values approaching the value cap
stress.note: large magnitudes stress the pre-modulo 64-bit arithmetic
